---
title: "Survival Models — Kaplan-Meier, Cox, Tobit"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## This chapter is a stub — contributions welcome
Fill it in following the two full exemplars
([Summary Statistics](summary-statistics.qmd),
[ANCOVA](ancova.qmd)) and the
[topic template](https://github.com/OWNER/submission-transparency/blob/main/templates/topic-template.qmd).
See the checklist in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
:::

## What a reviewer wants

For a time-to-event endpoint: the Kaplan-Meier estimate (median survival,
landmark rates), and the adjusted **hazard ratio** from a Cox model with its CI.
Tobit regression covers a related but distinct case — a continuous outcome
**censored at a limit** (e.g. an assay lower limit of quantification).

## Suggested minimal approach

- **KM:** `survival::survfit(Surv(time, event) ~ TRT)` — the universal base for
  survival in R; report median and landmark survival with `summary()`.
- **Cox:** `survival::coxph(Surv(time, event) ~ TRT + covariate)`; report
  `exp(coef)` and check proportional hazards with `cox.zph()`.
- **Tobit:** `survival::survreg(Surv(y, y > LLOQ, type = "left") ~ ...)`
  (or `AER::tobit`), for a left-censored continuous outcome.

## Simulation to build

- Simulate event times from a Weibull/exponential model with a **known** hazard
  ratio and independent censoring. Confirm the Cox `log(HR)` is approximately
  unbiased with ~95% coverage; note the known small-sample bias of the partial-
  likelihood estimate.
- For Tobit, simulate a Gaussian outcome left-censored at a known limit and show
  that Tobit recovers the true mean/slope while naive substitution
  (e.g. LLOQ/2) does not.
